_comment: Expiring exceptions for the frontend npm-audit gate (scripts/audit-gate.mjs).
POLICY (also in docs/GAPS.md): the gate STAYS BLOCKING — it never goes
warn-only. An exception is a TIME-BOXED, JUSTIFIED, OWNED deferral, not an
- A non-optional PRODUCTION dependency advisory gets NO exception — the
hard bar. It must be fixed, not deferred.
- Only dev / build-chain / optional-and-unused advisories are
exception-eligible, and only with a written exposure justification.
- expiry is 30 days by default, 90 days maximum. An EXPIRED exception is a
RED gate (the build fails), not a stale ignore — so the debt is revisited
Each entry: id (GHSA/advisory), package, justification (grounded in OUR
exposure, not the advisory's generic text), owner, added, expires (YYYY-MM-DD).
A window over 30 days (the default) also needs 'window_reason'.
exposure, not the advisory's generic text), owner, added, expires (YYYY-MM-DD).

exceptions.justification: sharp is an OPTIONAL dependency of next (server-side next/image). This dashboard uses next/image nowhere (no , no next/image import, no ), next.config sets images.unoptimized so Next never invokes sharp, AND outputFileTracingExcludes drops sharp + its @img libvips natives from the .next/standalone bundle — verified absent from the build. The production image ships only .next/standalone, so the vulnerable libvips code is neither present in the signed artifact nor reachable at runtime. sharp stays in the CI/dev tree only (npm --omit=optional also removes Tailwind's lightningcss and Next's SWC natives, breaking the build), which is why the audit still sees it. Retire when next ships a patched sharp (>=0.35.0).
exceptions.window_reason: 90d (the max) because the fix is out of our hands — it waits on Next to ship a patched sharp (>=0.35.0). The exposure is verified nil (sharp absent from the shipped .next/standalone bundle), so a shorter revisit cadence would only churn the expiry with no action available.
